candidate_rows.conclusion: Adjacent log-concavity is satisfied by the rational countermodel but does not prevent larger contiguous Jensen-window PF failures.
candidate_rows.next_action: Do not use adjacent log-concavity alone as a bridge lemma.
candidate_rows.proof_boundary: Ratio-condition theorem-search diagnostic only; not a proof of Jensen-window PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
candidate_rows.title: adjacent log-concavity

candidate_rows.conclusion: Monotone contraction of the adjacent ratios is still satisfied by the rational countermodel.
candidate_rows.next_action: Require a stronger structural condition than monotone contraction variables.
candidate_rows.proof_boundary: Ratio-condition theorem-search diagnostic only; not a proof of Jensen-window PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
candidate_rows.title: decreasing adjacent ratio contractions

candidate_rows.conclusion: The natural two-log-concavity inequality is positive at the countermodel but does not prevent Jensen-window PF failure.
candidate_rows.next_action: Do not promote finite higher log-concavity of A_0..A_4 into the missing all-minor theorem.
candidate_rows.proof_boundary: Ratio-condition theorem-search diagnostic only; not a proof of Jensen-window PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
candidate_rows.title: second-order log-concavity of the original five-term prefix

candidate_rows.conclusion: The selected hard formulas are all certified, but this selected family misses larger contiguous failures.

candidate_rows.next_action: Use the frontier scout before treating selected low-degree positivity as structural evidence.

candidate_rows.conclusion: This condition would rule out the degree-3 countermodel, but it is the degree-3 hyperbolicity target itself, not a bridge from sign-regularity.

candidate_rows.next_action: Use only as a finite window consequence after a noncircular theorem proves it.

candidate_rows.conclusion: This condition would rule out the degree-4 countermodel, but it is a window hyperbolicity condition, not a structural bridge.

candidate_rows.next_action: Use only as a finite window consequence after a noncircular theorem proves it.

candidate_rows.conclusion: A positive rational extension satisfies this stronger contraction condition while preserving negative frontier minors, so the condition is not a standalone bridge.
candidate_rows.next_action: Do not use contraction log-concavity alone as a bridge lemma; it can only appear inside a stronger noncircular theorem.
candidate_rows.proof_boundary: Ratio-condition theorem-search diagnostic only; not a proof of Jensen-window PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

invariants: Rejected rows are satisfied by the countermodel and still have negative target failures.
Rows rejected by constructed extension must include positive condition values and negative target failures at that extension.
Tautological window rows are not allowed to close jwpf_06 as bridge theorems.
Open candidate rows are not promoted without a source theorem and frontier-minor proof.
proof_boundary: Ratio-condition theorem-search diagnostic only; not a proof of a positive kernel, Cauchy-Binet identity, Jensen-window PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

summary.main_finding: Adjacent log-concavity, decreasing contraction variables, second-order log-concavity, and selected low-degree Bernstein positivity are all insufficient bridge conditions; the rational countermodel satisfies them while the frontier minors d3 m=8 and d4 m=6 are negative.  The remaining contraction log-concavity candidate is rejected by a positive rational extension with the same frontier failures.
